purpose: Agent-readable component inventory for selecting, wiring, and documenting Control Surface UI components without reading source files.

selectionOrder: Choose one or more recipes by recipe id
Copy required classes and data attributes
Wire optional JavaScript APIs only if behavior is required
Listen to emitted events for host integration
Document the component choice using docs/agentic-ergonomics.md

lifecycle.esm: import { init, destroy } from 'control-surface-ui'; init(root); destroy(root);
lifecycle.partialHydration: Use init(fragment, { modules: ['icons', 'tables'] }) after injection and destroy(fragment, { modules: ['tables'] }) before removal.

programmaticBehavior.controller: Use InterfaceFramework.getComponentController(target) for open, close, toggle, select, setState, reset, refresh, and destroy across component families.
programmaticBehavior.rule: Programmatic actions must travel through the same behavior paths as pointer and keyboard actions so ARIA state, events, and visual classes stay synchronized.

states.label: Ready
states.meaning: Component has reusable markup, tokenized styling, documented APIs/events, accessible state, examples, and passing static behavior contracts.

states.label: Hardening
states.meaning: Component is usable but still needs production fixtures, CI budget evidence, release provenance, or accepted-risk documentation.

components.name: Shell And Layout
whenToUse: Creating a full application page with topbar, sidebar, content, or right-side detail regions.
Creating a documentation or design-system surface that needs framework navigation.
avoidWhen: Only a small embedded widget is needed inside a host page.
accessibility: Use one landmark header and one main region per page.
Use aria-label on sidebars and detail panels when their visible heading is not adjacent.

contract.variantCoverage: docs/component-api.md variant matrix or component-specific API section
contract.copyPasteCoverage: docs/component-api.md copy-paste example or linked recipe

stateAndMotion.policy: Shell motion is limited to tokenized color, density, and region visibility transitions and must respect prefers-reduced-motion.
stateAndMotion.reducedMotion: Use @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) and immediate state synchronization for users who opt out of motion.
stateAndMotion.agentInstruction: Select this family as motion-ready when these hooks and states are sufficient; otherwise add a component-specific state entry before shipping.

components.name: Topbar And Utility Cluster
whenToUse: Adding enterprise search, notification, and account controls to the top-right page context.
Making navigation route-aware with a compact active underline style.
avoidWhen: The surface is public marketing content with a separate site navigation rhythm.
accessibility: Keep popover toggles as native buttons with aria-expanded and aria-controls.
Use aria-current='page' for the active route link.

contract.variantCoverage: docs/component-api.md variant matrix or component-specific API section
contract.copyPasteCoverage: docs/component-api.md copy-paste example or linked recipe

stateAndMotion.policy: Utility-cluster state changes travel through the popover, route, and autocomplete APIs so keyboard, ARIA, and visual transition state stay synchronized.
stateAndMotion.reducedMotion: Use @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) and immediate state synchronization for users who opt out of motion.
stateAndMotion.agentInstruction: Select this family as motion-ready when these hooks and states are sufficient; otherwise add a component-specific state entry before shipping.

components.name: Sidebar And Navigation Lists
whenToUse: Creating filters, library sections, saved views, watchlists, or route groups.
Creating dense left navigation with optional counts and section headers.
avoidWhen: The target screen has no persistent page-level navigation or filters.
accessibility: Give sidebars an accessible name when they contain navigation.
Do not use links for controls that only toggle filters.

contract.variantCoverage: docs/component-api.md variant matrix or component-specific API section
contract.copyPasteCoverage: docs/component-api.md copy-paste example or linked recipe

stateAndMotion.policy: Navigation motion uses disclosure and route-state classes only; reduced-motion users receive immediate open/close state changes.
stateAndMotion.reducedMotion: Use @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) and immediate state synchronization for users who opt out of motion.
stateAndMotion.agentInstruction: Select this family as motion-ready when these hooks and states are sufficient; otherwise add a component-specific state entry before shipping.

components.name: Buttons, Icon Buttons, And Command Strips
whenToUse: Adding command hierarchy, review actions, toolbar actions, destructive confirmations, or dense icon-only controls.
Creating visual anchors for table rows, graph toolbars, and agent controls.
avoidWhen: A text link is more appropriate for simple navigation.
accessibility: Icon-only buttons require aria-label.
Destructive buttons should be visually and semantically distinct.

contract.variantCoverage: docs/component-api.md variant matrix or component-specific API section
contract.copyPasteCoverage: docs/component-api.md copy-paste example or linked recipe

stateAndMotion.policy: Action controls expose state hooks for demo and host code while preserving native button semantics and reduced-motion-safe transitions.
stateAndMotion.reducedMotion: Use @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) and immediate state synchronization for users who opt out of motion.
stateAndMotion.agentInstruction: Select this family as motion-ready when these hooks and states are sufficient; otherwise add a component-specific state entry before shipping.

components.name: Split Buttons And Menus
whenToUse: A primary command has alternate formats or modes, such as export CSV, JSON, or PDF.
A compact toolbar needs a keyboard-accessible overflow or row action menu.
avoidWhen: All actions are equally important; use a command strip instead.
accessibility: Menu toggle must expose aria-expanded.
Menu items use roving tabindex and close on Escape.

contract.variantCoverage: docs/component-api.md variant matrix or component-specific API section
contract.copyPasteCoverage: docs/component-api.md copy-paste example or linked recipe

stateAndMotion.policy: Split-button menu transitions are driven by the same menu state used by keyboard roving, escape-stack cleanup, and action events.
stateAndMotion.reducedMotion: Use @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) and immediate state synchronization for users who opt out of motion.
stateAndMotion.agentInstruction: Select this family as motion-ready when these hooks and states are sufficient; otherwise add a component-specific state entry before shipping.

components.name: Icon System
whenToUse: A control, entity, organization, source, or provenance marker needs a consistent 24px outline glyph.
A design-system page needs a searchable icon catalog.
A diagram or card needs an approved SVG, PNG, JPG, WebP, AVIF, GIF, blob, or data URI mark with framework-managed sizing and fallback.
avoidWhen: A large decorative photograph should be treated as content media or a background rather than as a compact icon/asset slot.
accessibility: Use aria-hidden on decorative icons.
Pair meaningful icons with labels or accessible names.
Asset slots should use empty alt text for decorative marks and descriptive alt text when the mark carries information.

contract.variantCoverage: docs/component-api.md variant matrix or component-specific API section
contract.copyPasteCoverage: docs/component-api.md copy-paste example or linked recipe

stateAndMotion.policy: Icons inherit tokenized currentColor state by default; larger domain seals keep motion to hover/focus emphasis and never animate essential meaning.
stateAndMotion.reducedMotion: Use @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) and immediate state synchronization for users who opt out of motion.
stateAndMotion.agentInstruction: Select this family as motion-ready when these hooks and states are sufficient; otherwise add a component-specific state entry before shipping.

components.name: Theme Controls
whenToUse: A page or profile/account menu needs light, dark, high-contrast, system, or scoped theme switching.
A component showcase needs theme preview controls.
avoidWhen: The host application owns all theme state and will only consume tokens.
accessibility: Default theme is light.
High contrast is a first-class option, not a decorative variant.

contract.variantCoverage: docs/component-api.md variant matrix or component-specific API section
contract.copyPasteCoverage: docs/component-api.md copy-paste example or linked recipe

components.name: Fields, Validation, Help Text, And Error Summaries
whenToUse: A form needs visible labels, help text, validation, error summaries, or reviewer decision reasons.
A host workflow needs field-state events for audit or telemetry.
avoidWhen: The control is purely display-only metadata.
accessibility: Every field must have a visible label.
Validation feedback must be wired through aria-describedby.

contract.variantCoverage: docs/component-api.md variant matrix or component-specific API section
contract.copyPasteCoverage: docs/component-api.md copy-paste example or linked recipe

components.name: Search And Autocomplete
whenToUse: A search box needs local or server-side suggestions with highlighted matches.
A global search, table filter, or public-site search should expose keyboard-accessible suggestions.
avoidWhen: The result set is too sensitive to expose suggestions before explicit submit.
accessibility: Input behaves as a combobox with active-descendant management.
Escape closes suggestions without clearing the query.

contract.variantCoverage: docs/component-api.md variant matrix or component-specific API section
contract.copyPasteCoverage: docs/component-api.md copy-paste example or linked recipe

components.name: Upload And Dropzone
whenToUse: A page needs artifact staging for PDFs, DOCX, CSV, JSON, source packages, or parser inputs.
Users need drag/drop, click-to-browse, selected-file feedback, and host upload events.
avoidWhen: The upload is a background-only API integration with no user-visible artifact staging.
accessibility: Dropzones must retain a native file input.
Keyboard activation on Enter or Space should open the file picker.

contract.variantCoverage: docs/component-api.md variant matrix or component-specific API section
contract.copyPasteCoverage: docs/component-api.md copy-paste example or linked recipe

components.name: Command Palette
whenToUse: A dense product surface needs searchable action discovery, keyboard shortcuts, or cross-page commands.
An agentic workflow needs a single command surface for open graph, create review task, export, assign, or navigate actions.
avoidWhen: Only two or three local actions exist; use a toolbar or split button.
accessibility: Ctrl+K opens the first palette when present.
Arrow keys move through visible commands and Enter activates the active item.

contract.variantCoverage: docs/component-api.md variant matrix or component-specific API section
contract.copyPasteCoverage: docs/component-api.md copy-paste example or linked recipe

components.name: Editable Grid
whenToUse: Extracted line items, claim candidates, obligation owners, or confidence values need lightweight inline editing.
A full spreadsheet is unnecessary but table cells need dirty-state and change events.
avoidWhen: The grid requires formula engines, multi-sheet workbooks, or heavy spreadsheet semantics.
accessibility: Editable cells should keep native focus behavior.
Status text should summarize row count and changes.

contract.variantCoverage: docs/component-api.md variant matrix or component-specific API section
contract.copyPasteCoverage: docs/component-api.md copy-paste example or linked recipe

components.name: Date And Calendar Picker
whenToUse: Review due dates, source windows, deadlines, or publication windows need compact date selection.
A host date library is not required for a simple single-date control.
avoidWhen: The application needs recurring rules, timezone scheduling, or complex range calendars.
accessibility: Selected date must be visible in a text field or status row.
Calendar buttons expose pressed state and a roving tab stop.
Native date input remains available for mobile and assistive fallback.

contract.variantCoverage: docs/component-api.md variant matrix or component-specific API section
contract.copyPasteCoverage: docs/component-api.md copy-paste example or linked recipe

stateAndMotion.policy: Calendar motion follows popover state and selected-date state; reduced-motion users receive immediate open, close, and selection feedback.
stateAndMotion.reducedMotion: Use @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) and immediate state synchronization for users who opt out of motion.
stateAndMotion.agentInstruction: Select this family as motion-ready when these hooks and states are sufficient; otherwise add a component-specific state entry before shipping.

components.name: Wizard And Stepper
whenToUse: A watchlist, rule builder, ingest setup, export flow, or review activation needs multi-step progress.
Users should move forward/backward or jump to previous completed steps.
avoidWhen: All controls must be scanned simultaneously; use a panel stack or form section layout.
accessibility: Use aria-current=step on the active step.
Step status must be visible outside color alone.
Arrow keys, Home, End, Enter, and Space should move or activate steps without losing the roving tab stop.
Semantic variants require readable labels or metadata for completed, active, future, blocked, and optional states.
Configuration controls that switch boxed/unboxed variants must keep aria-pressed synchronized.

contract.variantCoverage: docs/component-api.md variant matrix or component-specific API section
contract.copyPasteCoverage: docs/component-api.md copy-paste example or linked recipe

components.name: Annotation Toolbar
whenToUse: A document, claim, parser, or review surface needs CLM, REF, ORG, EVD, IMP, or ENB markup modes.
Reviewers need a compact toolbar for selecting semantic highlight classes.
avoidWhen: Annotations are read-only and do not need mode switching.
accessibility: Toolbar buttons use aria-pressed for active mode.
Arrow keys, Home, End, Enter, and Space use a roving tab stop across tools.
Highlight previews include text labels, not color alone.
Abbreviated tool buttons should provide data-if-annotation-label and data-if-annotation-description.

contract.variantCoverage: docs/component-api.md variant matrix or component-specific API section
contract.copyPasteCoverage: docs/component-api.md copy-paste example or linked recipe

components.name: Empty, Loading, Success, Cancelled, And Error States
whenToUse: A table, adapter, search result, document artifact, upload area, or graph panel needs recoverable success/empty/loading/error/cancelled states.
A page needs consistent skeleton loading, no-results, retry, or degraded data feedback.
avoidWhen: A destructive failure requires a modal confirmation or support handoff.
accessibility: State panels must use text and actions, not icons alone.
Loading states should avoid trapping keyboard focus.

contract.variantCoverage: docs/component-api.md variant matrix or component-specific API section
contract.copyPasteCoverage: docs/component-api.md copy-paste example or linked recipe

components.name: Tabs And Accordions
whenToUse: Related content needs scoped navigation inside a detail panel.
Sections need disclosure without leaving the page.
avoidWhen: Users must compare all sections at once; use panels or split views instead.
accessibility: Tabs require role='tablist', role='tab', role='tabpanel', aria-controls, and aria-labelledby.
Accordion triggers should expose aria-expanded and aria-controls.

contract.variantCoverage: docs/component-api.md variant matrix or component-specific API section
contract.copyPasteCoverage: docs/component-api.md copy-paste example or linked recipe

components.name: Popovers, Notifications, Account Menus, Modals, Drawers, And Toasts
whenToUse: A focused decision, account surface, notification inbox, contextual detail, or transient confirmation is needed.
A detail panel must open without navigating away.
avoidWhen: The content is a primary workflow that deserves a full page or persistent pane.
accessibility: Modals and drawers must trap focus and close with Escape.
Popovers need aria-expanded and aria-controls on the trigger.

contract.variantCoverage: docs/component-api.md variant matrix or component-specific API section
contract.copyPasteCoverage: docs/component-api.md copy-paste example or linked recipe

stateAndMotion.policy: Overlay motion is controlled by the escape stack and focus manager; open/close transitions never delay ARIA state or focus restoration.
stateAndMotion.reducedMotion: Use @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) and immediate state synchronization for users who opt out of motion.
stateAndMotion.agentInstruction: Select this family as motion-ready when these hooks and states are sufficient; otherwise add a component-specific state entry before shipping.

components.name: Enterprise Data Table
whenToUse: Displaying records that require sorting, filtering, pagination, selection, expansion, resizing, pinning, virtualization, or server adapters.
Creating source registries, review queues, search results, or sibling comparisons.
avoidWhen: Fewer than three simple facts can be represented as a metadata panel or card list.
accessibility: Table controls must be buttons or inputs, not clickable cells alone.
Virtualized tables must keep range and total status visible.

contract.variantCoverage: docs/component-api.md variant matrix or component-specific API section
contract.copyPasteCoverage: docs/component-api.md copy-paste example or linked recipe

components.name: Operations Workspace
whenToUse: A dense analytics or operations page needs selectable signal cards that drive drilldown panels, tables, provenance, and record detail.
A workspace needs to combine KPI cards, table command bands, field-level provenance, source health, action queues, and right-side decision support.
avoidWhen: The page only needs a static dashboard with no selected signal, record detail, or cross-surface behavior.
A strict entity graph or tree is the primary interaction model; choose graph-explorer or hierarchy-explorer instead.
accessibility: Signal controls must be keyboard-focusable buttons with synchronized selected state.
Use visible labels and panel headings so a selected signal does not rely on color alone.
Escape should reset the active signal when focus is inside the operations workspace.

contract.variantCoverage: docs/component-api.md#operations-workspace
contract.copyPasteCoverage: docs/component-api.md#operations-workspace
contract.eventPolicy: emits if:operations-signal-change and if:operations-signal-reset for pointer, keyboard, and JavaScript state changes

stateAndMotion.reducedMotion: Selection and panel state must update immediately; transitions are visual polish only.
stateAndMotion.agentInstruction: Choose this family for dense operational workspaces that need synchronized signal cards, panels, provenance, actions, and table command areas.

components.name: Charts, Analytics, And Sparklines
whenToUse: A dashboard, source table, design-system demo, or analytical surface needs dependency-free bar, line, pie, heatmap, funnel, scatter, treemap, gauge, bullet, histogram, or sparkline visualizations.
Small trendlines need to live inside KPI cards or table cells.
avoidWhen: The application needs heavy statistical charting, canvas-scale datasets, or full BI interactions.
accessibility: Pair charts with visible labels, captions, and summarized data where possible.
Hover-only details must also be available on focus.

contract.variantCoverage: docs/component-api.md variant matrix or component-specific API section
contract.copyPasteCoverage: docs/component-api.md copy-paste example or linked recipe
contract.eventPolicy: emits if:chart-dataset for dataset swaps and if:chart-point-select when users or host code select a chart datum

stateAndMotion.policy: Chart motion is data-state driven and resumable; selected points expose aria-selected and emit host-consumable selection events. Reduced-motion mode snaps values without animated interpolation.
stateAndMotion.reducedMotion: Use @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) and immediate state synchronization for users who opt out of motion.
stateAndMotion.agentInstruction: Select this family as motion-ready when these hooks and states are sufficient; otherwise add a component-specific state entry before shipping.

components.name: Performance And Scale Lab
whenToUse: A design-system, release smoke page, or downstream sample needs to prove tables, graphs, diagrams, document viewers, and charts remain responsive with large synthetic data.
A host application needs a reusable overflow check that distinguishes page-breaking overflow from intentional internal scroll regions.
avoidWhen: You need statistically rigorous benchmarking; use browser performance tooling and CI metrics in addition to this smoke component.
Large data should be loaded from a real service; use production adapters and feed the results into the same component surfaces.
accessibility: Do not treat visual rendering speed as the only pass condition; large demos still need keyboard focus, visible labels, and readable status.
Scrollable stress regions must be contained inside named panels so mobile users do not get page-wide horizontal overflow.

contract.variantCoverage: docs/component-api.md variant matrix or component-specific API section
contract.copyPasteCoverage: docs/component-api.md copy-paste example or linked recipe

stateAndMotion.policy: Scale-lab motion reports live state without layout shift; long-running runs expose cancel/retry state and respect reduced-motion preferences.
stateAndMotion.reducedMotion: Use @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) and immediate state synchronization for users who opt out of motion.
stateAndMotion.agentInstruction: Select this family as motion-ready when these hooks and states are sufficient; otherwise add a component-specific state entry before shipping.

components.name: KPI And Dashboard Metrics
whenToUse: A dashboard needs dense scan units with icon, label, value, delta, trendline, and metadata row.
A compact metric needs source/date context for credibility.
avoidWhen: The value cannot be summarized safely as a single number or status.
accessibility: Do not rely on color alone for positive or negative deltas.
Metric metadata should be visible, not tooltip-only.

contract.variantCoverage: docs/component-api.md variant matrix or component-specific API section
contract.copyPasteCoverage: docs/component-api.md copy-paste example or linked recipe
contract.eventPolicy: emits if:kpi-metric-update when metric value, delta, trendline, or streaming sparkline state changes

stateAndMotion.policy: KPI state changes are compact and scan-safe: value, label, delta, metadata, and sparkline update as one dense scan unit.
stateAndMotion.reducedMotion: Use @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) and immediate state synchronization for users who opt out of motion.
stateAndMotion.agentInstruction: Select this family as motion-ready when these hooks and states are sufficient; otherwise add a component-specific state entry before shipping.

components.name: Metadata Panels
whenToUse: An entity needs provenance, confidence, source, owner, date, review state, or linked metadata facts.
A detail pane must summarize an item without forcing navigation.
avoidWhen: The relationship structure is primary; use graph, hierarchy, or relationship map components.
accessibility: Use definition-list semantics or visible labels for key-value facts.
Keep confidence labels textual in addition to meter visuals.

contract.variantCoverage: docs/component-api.md variant matrix or component-specific API section
contract.copyPasteCoverage: docs/component-api.md copy-paste example or linked recipe

components.name: Collapsible Cards And Panels
whenToUse: A card, panel, metadata section, or design-system specimen needs optional inline details that can be hidden without changing routes.
A host app needs to persist expanded/collapsed section state as part of workspace preferences.
avoidWhen: The surface should become a larger full-screen work area; use surface expansion instead.
The content is mutually exclusive navigation; use tabs or accordions.
accessibility: Use a native button for the toggle.
Behavior must sync aria-expanded and aria-controls to the hidden region.
Keep collapsed state optional; do not hide required workflow decisions by default.

contract.variantCoverage: docs/component-api.md variant matrix or component-specific API section
contract.copyPasteCoverage: docs/component-api.md copy-paste example or linked recipe

components.name: Graph Explorer
whenToUse: Users need to traverse policies, sources, organizations, obligations, evidence, gaps, events, and relationship types.
A dense network needs selectable nodes, edge labels, filters, clusters, panning, zooming, and external layout engines.
avoidWhen: The relationship is a simple ordered chain; use relationship map or hierarchy instead.
accessibility: Provide data-if-graph-a11y fallback for keyboard and non-visual traversal.
Node and edge labels must be buttons when they are interactive.

contract.variantCoverage: docs/component-api.md variant matrix or component-specific API section
contract.copyPasteCoverage: docs/component-api.md copy-paste example or linked recipe

components.name: Relationship Maps And Authority Chains
whenToUse: A compact authority path, impact path, or lineage chain needs directional connectors.
Showing summarized upstream/downstream relationships below a graph or detail panel.
avoidWhen: Users need to drag nodes, filter edge types, or explore clusters; use graph explorer.
accessibility: Describe relationship direction in aria-label or visible connector text.
Avoid raw ASCII arrows in final UI.

contract.variantCoverage: docs/component-api.md variant matrix or component-specific API section
contract.copyPasteCoverage: docs/component-api.md copy-paste example or linked recipe

stateAndMotion.policy: Relationship-map motion treats lines, arrows, labels, and nodes as a single semantic edge contract so hover/focus never separates visual meaning from the relationship.
stateAndMotion.reducedMotion: Use @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) and immediate state synchronization for users who opt out of motion.
stateAndMotion.agentInstruction: Select this family as motion-ready when these hooks and states are sufficient; otherwise add a component-specific state entry before shipping.

components.name: Hierarchy Explorer
whenToUse: Representing organization hierarchy, policy authority hierarchy, law-to-implementation drilldown, branch rollups, or landscape anatomy.
Users need expandable parent-child disclosure with detail panels.
avoidWhen: Relationships are many-to-many rather than hierarchical; use graph explorer.
accessibility: Use role='tree' or clear grouped lists for deep hierarchies.
Branch toggles must have aria-expanded and an explicit label.
Landscape connector labels must be visible text or mirrored in the related node accessible names.

contract.variantCoverage: docs/component-api.md variant matrix or component-specific API section
contract.copyPasteCoverage: docs/component-api.md copy-paste example or linked recipe

components.name: Claim Tracking And Status Timelines
whenToUse: A document parser extracts claims, obligations, references, implementation statements, or evidence needs that must be reviewed and completed.
A user needs to see whether line-item items have completed, failed, or are blocked.
avoidWhen: The only requirement is a passive historical timeline; use item history viewer.
accessibility: Each status step needs visible text, not color alone.
Claims that jump to documents should identify the target line or section.

contract.variantCoverage: docs/component-api.md variant matrix or component-specific API section
contract.copyPasteCoverage: docs/component-api.md copy-paste example or linked recipe

components.name: Item History Viewer
whenToUse: A policy, source, claim, finding, or organization needs version history, source history, decision history, or field-change history.
Users need to inspect the before/after impact of a change.
avoidWhen: The primary story is completion status, not historical events; use claim tracking.
accessibility: Event selection should be reachable by keyboard.
Current event state should be visible in text.

contract.variantCoverage: docs/component-api.md variant matrix or component-specific API section
contract.copyPasteCoverage: docs/component-api.md copy-paste example or linked recipe

stateAndMotion.policy: History-viewer transitions preserve chronological scan order and never hide surrounding context from keyboard or reduced-motion users.
stateAndMotion.reducedMotion: Use @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) and immediate state synchronization for users who opt out of motion.
stateAndMotion.agentInstruction: Select this family as motion-ready when these hooks and states are sufficient; otherwise add a component-specific state entry before shipping.

components.name: Document Viewer And Reconstituted Text
whenToUse: Viewing ingested artifacts, embedded originals, reconstituted text, parser output, semantic highlighting, references, organizations, and claims.
A user needs full-text search, clickable highlights, and a section map.
avoidWhen: Only a single source citation needs to be shown; use metadata panel or source card.
accessibility: Line numbers and annotation labels should not disrupt reading order.
Annotation tooltips must also be reachable by focus or click.

contract.variantCoverage: docs/component-api.md variant matrix or component-specific API section
contract.copyPasteCoverage: docs/component-api.md copy-paste example or linked recipe

components.name: Policy Diff Review
whenToUse: Comparing policy versions, obligations, line changes, or proposed edits side by side.
A review workflow needs approve/reject/escalate decisions tied to changed lines.
avoidWhen: The comparison is purely metadata-level; use item history viewer.
accessibility: Added and removed states need text labels, not only red/green.
Next/previous controls should announce current change count.
Change rows use roving focus with Arrow, Home, End, Enter, and Space support.

contract.variantCoverage: docs/component-api.md variant matrix or component-specific API section
contract.copyPasteCoverage: docs/component-api.md copy-paste example or linked recipe

components.name: Reviewer Workflow
whenToUse: A queue of findings needs roving selection, shortcut decisions, decision reason capture, counts, and ledger entries.
Analysts need approve, reject, escalate, assign, snooze, or reopen controls.
avoidWhen: The item is not a human-in-the-loop review workflow.
accessibility: Document keyboard shortcuts near the workflow or in help.
Do not require shortcut keys for completion; buttons must remain available.

contract.variantCoverage: docs/component-api.md variant matrix or component-specific API section
contract.copyPasteCoverage: docs/component-api.md copy-paste example or linked recipe

components.name: Architecture Diagram Primitives
whenToUse: Reconstructing architecture diagrams, flow diagrams, topology maps, swimlanes, trust boundaries, or dependency matrices using framework components.
Diagram boxes need hover/focus/click details, layer toggles, sliders, connector routing, and export.
Diagram nodes need either framework glyphs or embedded SVG/PNG/JPG/etc. service marks with consistent resizing.
avoidWhen: The diagram is an arbitrary image with no interactive component model.
accessibility: Diagram nodes must be keyboard-selectable if they reveal details.
Provide text summaries for complex architecture diagrams.
Diagram search must expose result count text and keep matched text visible inside focused nodes.
Edit mode must keep draggable nodes keyboard-selectable and expose layout changes through events/adapters.

contract.copyPasteCoverage: docs/component-api.md copy-paste example or linked recipe

components.name: Governance And Operations Patterns
whenToUse: Showing agent health, run evaluation, provenance ledgers, degraded states, export/API contracts, alert rules, role-gated actions, or publication thresholds.
An operations page needs healthy, warning, degraded, paused, running, blocked, and failing states.
avoidWhen: The item is a normal record table without operational state.
accessibility: Every operational state must have visible text.
Progress bars and meters need adjacent value labels.

contract.variantCoverage: docs/component-api.md variant matrix or component-specific API section
contract.copyPasteCoverage: docs/component-api.md copy-paste example or linked recipe

components.name: Configurable Showcases
whenToUse: A design-system page needs live sliders, transitions, state toggles, theme demonstrations, or configurable component previews.
A host app needs token-controlled local customization without custom JavaScript.
avoidWhen: Configuration is purely server-side and should not alter live CSS variables.
accessibility: Slider outputs should update visible output elements.
State buttons should expose active state with aria-pressed when mutually exclusive.

contract.variantCoverage: docs/component-api.md variant matrix or component-specific API section
contract.copyPasteCoverage: docs/component-api.md copy-paste example or linked recipe

components.name: Public Website Patterns
whenToUse: Building Adam Boas Consulting style pages: homepage, services, profile, resume, blog, publications, contact, reference loops, public search, or engagement packages.
A public site should use the framework token system without the dense enterprise shell.
avoidWhen: The page is an internal product workspace; use shell-layout and product components.
accessibility: Public pages should keep headings in document order.
Media and attribution should include source or alt text where relevant.
Public search filters should expose aria-pressed and empty states should be announced near the result set.

contract.variantCoverage: docs/component-api.md variant matrix or component-specific API section
contract.copyPasteCoverage: docs/component-api.md copy-paste example or linked recipe

stateAndMotion.policy: Public-site motion uses the same route, disclosure, and search-highlight hooks as the application shell so marketing pages stay framework-compatible.
stateAndMotion.reducedMotion: Use @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) and immediate state synchronization for users who opt out of motion.
stateAndMotion.agentInstruction: Select this family as motion-ready when these hooks and states are sufficient; otherwise add a component-specific state entry before shipping.

recipes.title: Application shell with topbar, utility cluster, sidebar, and content region
Data table with sorting, filtering, pagination, selection, resize, pinning, and server adapter
Graph explorer with filters, traversal, clusters, node details, panning, zooming, and layout adapters
Artifact viewer with embedded source, reconstituted text, semantic highlights, claims, refs, and orgs
Architecture diagram with stages, services, connectors, details, sliders, layers, and export
Coverage closure component suite for P0/P1 gaps
Searchable command palette with keyboard activation
Compact date/calendar picker for due dates
Semantic annotation toolbar for document review
Empty, loading, and error state variants

stabilityTiers.stable: Public component contract is ready for downstream Policy MVP or AdamBoas.com planning and buildout. Markup, classes, data attributes, APIs, events or explicit static-event policy, accessibility notes, examples, and docs are required.
stabilityTiers.experimental: Component is framework-owned and reusable, but production adoption must keep the documented fallback, adapter boundary, and hardening work item visible.
stabilityTiers.demo-only: Component exists to test or demonstrate framework capabilities and should not be treated as a production surface without promotion.

stableComponentRequirements: emitted events or explicit static event policy
